title: fagraea cochinchinensis
intro: Fagraea cochinchinensis, also known as the dog wood tree or fragrant frangipani, is a flowering plant species native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Fagraea cochinchinensis is typically prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea to utilize its medicinal properties.
